question.text: Which of the following correctly describes a 'call option' in commodity derivatives?

answer.text: A contract giving the purchaser the right to buy a specified quantity of a commodity at a particular price on or before a future date
A contract obligating the purchaser to buy a specified quantity of a commodity at a particular price on a future date
A contract giving the purchaser the right to sell a specified quantity of a commodity at a particular price on or before a future date
A contract obligating the seller to buy back the commodity at the exercise price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A call option gives the purchaser the RIGHT (not obligation) to BUY a specified quantity at the exercise price on or before the expiration date. **The Trap:** Option (c) describes a put option. Options (b) and (d) incorrectly state obligations for the buyer — the buyer has rights, the seller has obligations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII, I, and IV all test the call/put distinction and rights vs obligations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: Call = right to Buy, Put = right to Sell. The buyer pays premium for this right; the seller receives premium and bears the obligation.
